# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>
You are a repo maintainer enforcing a commit metadata policy. You have a repository at `/home/user/repo` which is already initialized with `jj`. Your task is to:
1. Configure `jj` locally in this repository to use a custom template for `jj log` by default. The template should output only the commit's short ID, the author's email, and the first line of the description, separated by ` | ` (e.g., `abc1234 | user@example.com | Fix bug`).
2. Create a new commit with the description "Update metadata policy" and ensure it is the current working copy.
3. Run `jj log -r @` and save the output to `/home/user/log_output.txt`.

todos.content: Inspect /home/user/repo and existing jj configuration
Configure local jj log template in /home/user/repo
Create commit with description 'Update metadata policy' and make it current working copy
Run jj log -r @ and save output to /home/user/log_output.txt

results.content:
jj 0.38.0-2508982cde5c7e4db0933e0b6469f9e778e71e28
operation.hostname = "3492213f-9a18-4d2e-8d2f-4f5fc89ad1bd"
operation.username = "root"
user.name = "Test User"
user.email = "test@example.com"

todos.content: Inspect /home/user/repo and existing jj configuration
Configure local jj log template in /home/user/repo
Create commit with description 'Update metadata policy' and make it current working copy
Run jj log -r @ and save output to /home/user/log_output.txt

arguments.command: jj config set --repo templates.log 'change_id.shortest(7) ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ " | " ++ description.first_line()'

results.content: {'error': "ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/home/user/repo/.jj/repo/config.toml'"}

arguments.command: find .jj -maxdepth 3 -type f | sort && echo '---' && jj config list --repo

todos.content: Inspect /home/user/repo and existing jj configuration

arguments.command: jj describe -m 'Update metadata policy' && jj status && jj log -r @
